steps.title: Identify diameter AC
steps.content: The diagram marks angle $ABC$ as a right angle. An inscribed right angle subtends a diameter, so $AC$ is a diameter of the circle. Since the diameter is $175$, $$AD+CD=175.$$

steps.title: Use the altitude-to-hypotenuse theorem
steps.content: Because $AC\perp BE$ at $D$, segment $BD$ is the altitude from the right angle to hypotenuse $AC$ in right triangle $ABC$. Therefore: $$BD^2=AD\cdot CD.$$ With $BD=\sqrt{346}$, this gives $$AD\cdot CD=346.$$

steps.title: Solve for the two hypotenuse segments
steps.content: The two positive numbers $AD$ and $CD$ have sum $175$ and product $346$, so they are the roots of $$t^2-175t+346=0.$$ Factoring gives $t=2$ and $t=173$. Since $AB\lt BC$, the segment adjacent to $AB$ is shorter, so $AD=2$ and $CD=173$. Thus $$r=\frac{CD}{AD}=\frac{173}{2}.$$
